lines.target: David Wilson travels to work every morning by tube.
lines.base: David Wilson va (viaja) a trabajar todas las mañanas en (por) metro.
lines.pronunciation: .. tiud. — 2 ándegraund... — 3 anlail a i ns -. éndegraund... e. aik... — 4 ... ákoordintu... dsstanss =F siison tikét... álous... pilriód... 6 ooldzou.., Emplois. Shox ¢ .— 7 ... dehórni. — 8 ... colsou... viu. — 9 ... dabél... dekérs...

lines.target: The tube—or the Underground—is something like the Metro in Paris.
lines.base: El metro — o el « Underground » (subterráneo) es parecido (algo como) el metro de (en) París.

lines.target: But, unlike the Metro, it is rather old-fashioned and quite expensive.
lines.base: Pero a diferencia de éste, está un poco pasado de moda y es muy caro.

lines.target: You pay according to the distance you want to travel.
lines.base: Se paga conforme a la distancia que se quiera recorrer (viajar).

lines.target: You can buy* a season ticket, which allows you to travel for a certain period at a lower price.
lines.base: Se puede comprar un abono (billete de temporada) que permite viajar durante (por) un cierto período a precio más bajo.

lines.target: Although most of the tube is automatic, there are still employees who check your ticket at the exit.
lines.base: Aunque la mayor parte del metro está automatizado (es automático), aún hay empleados que revisan los billetes a la salida.

lines.target: So you must keep” your ticket until you finish your journey.
lines.base: Por tanto hay que guardar el billete hasta que se termine el viaje.

lines.target: You can also travel by bus, which is slower but gives* you a better view.
lines.base: También se puede ir (viajar) en autobús, que es más lento pero ofrece (da) un panorama (vista) mejor.

lines.target: Most buses are double-deckers and you are allowed to smoke upstairs.
lines.base: La mayor parte de los autobuses son de dos pisos y se permite fumar en la parte de arriba.

lines.target: On these buses, there is a driver and a conductor, who collects your fares.
lines.base: En estos autobuses, hay un conductor y un cobrador que recibe (recoge) el dinero (vuestras tarifas).

lines.target: Finally, there are the famous London taxis or “Hackney Cabs ”.
lines.base: Finalmente están los famosos taxis de Londres o Hackney cabs.

lines.target: These large, black, diesel-engined vehicles are a familiar sight in the Capital.
lines.base: Estos amplios vehículos negros, con motor diesel, son una visión familiar en la Capital.

lines.target: If the cab is free, you will see* a little “For Hire ” sign in the front.
lines.base: Si el taxi está libre, se ve una señal de «se alquila» en la parte delantera.

lines.target: Of course, the best way to see the city is on foot, but you need a rest from time to time.
lines.base: Por supuesto el mejor modo de ver la ciudad es a pie, pero hará falta un descanso de vez en cuando.

gap_candidates.target: David Wilson travels to work every morning by tube.
The tube—or the Underground—is something like the Metro in Paris.
But, unlike the Metro, it is rather old-fashioned and quite expensive. (1)
You pay according to the distance you want to travel. (2)
You can buy* a season ticket, which allows you to travel for a certain period at a lower price.
Although most of the tube is automatic, there are still employees who check your ticket at the exit. (3)
So you must keep” your ticket until you finish your journey. (4)
You can also travel by bus, which is slower but gives* you a better view.
Most buses are double-deckers and you are allowed to smoke upstairs. (5)
On these buses, there is a driver and a conductor, who collects your fares. (6)
Finally, there are the famous London taxis or “Hackney Cabs ”. (7)
These large, black, diesel-engined vehicles are a familiar sight in the Capital.
If the cab is free, you will see* a little “For Hire ” sign in the front. (8)
Of course, the best way to see the city is on foot, but you need a rest from time to time.
